description: §20.8.1 (V1 count ruling) — two `<outlet>` elements in mutually-exclusive if/else branches are STILL a duplicate. V1 = exactly one flat `<outlet>` per shell, counted STATICALLY across all edges (branch-exclusive / per-branch outlets are v1.next). The SYM outlet walker descends the markup if-chain `branches[].element` + `elseBranch` edges, so both outlets are counted and E-OUTLET-DUPLICATE fires. (Regression: the walker previously skipped if-chain branch edges, so branch-exclusive outlets were undercounted AND falsely tripped W-OUTLET-ABSENT.)
rationale: §20.8.1: 'V1 supports one flat `<outlet>` per shell.' Two outlets — even in disjoint if/else branches — exceed the one-static-outlet budget and fire E-OUTLET-DUPLICATE.
